explanation.rule: Adverbs ending in -ably or -ibly are formed from adjectives that end in -able or -ible. The adjective suffix changes to form the adverb.

groups.note: Use -ably for adjectives ending in -able (e.g. remarkable → remarkably), and -ibly for adjectives ending in -ible (e.g. responsible → responsibly). Sometimes the base verb changes before the suffix (e.g. notice → noticeably).

examples.definition: in a way that deserves respect and praise
by a large amount or to a great degree
in a sensible and trustworthy way
in a way that can be clearly seen
in a way that is surprising or impressive

wordForms.definition: in a way that deserves respect and praise
wordForms.example: She handled the difficult situation admirably.
The journey took considerably longer than expected.
wordForms.rootDefinition: large or important enough to be noticed

wordForms.definition: in a way that cannot be prevented or avoided
wordForms.example: Prices inevitably rose as demand increased.
wordForms.rootDefinition: certain to happen and impossible to avoid

wordForms.example: Several countries, notably France and Germany, opposed the proposal.
wordForms.rootDefinition: deserving to be noticed or mentioned

wordForms.definition: in a way that is easy to see or notice
wordForms.example: Her confidence had noticeably improved by the end of the course.
wordForms.rootDefinition: easy to see or be aware of

wordForms.definition: used to say that something is probably true
wordForms.example: You've presumably already heard the news about the merger.
wordForms.rootDefinition: able to be assumed or supposed

wordForms.definition: in a way that is surprising or impressively unusual
wordForms.example: She remained remarkably calm throughout the crisis.
wordForms.rootDefinition: unusual or impressive in a way that deserves notice

wordForms.definition: in a way that is easy to understand or excuse
wordForms.example: She was understandably upset after hearing the news.

wordForms.definition: in a way that can clearly be seen
wordForms.example: He was visibly shaken after the accident.

wordForms.definition: in a sensible and trustworthy way
wordForms.example: Students are expected to behave responsibly during field trips.
wordForms.rootDefinition: having a duty to deal with something; reliable

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: Prices ___ rose as the cost of raw materials increased.
The journey took ___ longer than we had planned.
She was ___ moved by the children's performance.
You have ___ already received the invitation by now.
She managed a ___ difficult workload without complaint.
He behaved ___ throughout the entire internship.

transformExercises.sentence: She was ___ disappointed when she heard the result. (UNDERSTAND)
The team performed ___ well given the circumstances. (REMARK)
Her performance had ___ improved since the last review. (NOTICE)
He dealt with the complaint ___ and earned praise from his manager. (ADMIRE)
Several teams, ___ the German side, dominated the competition. (NOTE)
